source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Network Security is Enabled

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Network Security is Enabled
input_row.description: In order to access Amazon Keyspaces the user is required to set specific networking parameters and security measurements without these extra steps they will not be able to access it. Users are required to create or select a virtual private cloud (VPC) and define their inbound and outbound rules accordingly.
input_row.rationale: Impact: Only authorized users have access to the database which limits and controls any risk of an attack. This ensures better performance of the system to a private network and better security.
input_row.audit: 1. Create or Select a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) • Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon VPC console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/vpc/. • Create a new VPC or select an existing VPC where you want to deploy your Amazon Keyspaces resources. 2. Configure Subnets • In the VPC console, navigate to Subnets in the left-side menu. • Create or select the subnets within your VPC where you want to deploy your Amazon Keyspaces resources. • Ensure you have private subnets to isolate your Keyspaces resources from the public internet. 3. Define Security Groups • In the VPC console, navigate to Security Groups in the left-side menu. • Create a new security group or select an existing one for your Amazon Keyspaces resources. • Configure inbound and outbound rules in the security group to control traffic access. o Allow inbound access only from trusted sources, such as specific IP ranges or security groups, on the necessary ports used by Amazon Keyspaces.  o Define outbound rules based on your requirements, allowing outbound traffic to necessary destinations or ports. • Associate the security group with your Amazon Keyspaces resources. 4. Configure Network Access Control Lists (ACLs) • In the VPC console, navigate to Network ACLs in the left-side menu. • Create or select the network ACLs associated with the subnets used by your Amazon Keyspaces resources. • Configure inbound and outbound rules in the network ACLs to control traffic access. o Define rules based on your security requirements, allowing only necessary protocols, ports, and IP ranges. o list text hereDeny unnecessary or unwanted traffic. • Associate the network ACLs with the subnets used by your Amazon Keyspaces resources. 5. Configure VPC Endpoints • In the VPC console, navigate to Endpoints in the left-side menu. • Create or select the VPC endpoints required for Amazon Keyspaces. • If you need to access Keyspaces from within your VPC, create a VPC endpoint for Amazon Keyspaces to connect your applications securely. • If you need to access Keyspaces from another VPC or on-premises network, set up VPC peering or a transit gateway to establish a secure connection. 6. Verify Connectivity and Test • Launch an Amazon EC2 instance within the same VPC and subnet as your Amazon Keyspaces resources or use an existing one. • Connect to the EC2 instance using SSH or other remote access methods. • Test the connectivity to your Amazon Keyspaces resources by trying to connect to them using the appropriate client or utility. • Verify that the network security settings allow the necessary traffic and deny unauthorized access.
input_row.impact: Only authorized users have access to the database which limits and controls any risk of an attack. This ensures better performance of the system to a private network and better security.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control "Ensure Network Security is Enabled" for Amazon Keyspaces involves configuring VPCs, subnets, security groups, network ACLs, and VPC endpoints. These are all standard network security configurations that can be verified using AWS APIs. Since these configurations are properties of the VPC service and can be queried through AWS APIs, boto3, or AWS CLI commands, the audit approach is Automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_vpc_network_security_keyspaces_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API Calls or boto3 SDK Methods:**
  - Use `boto3.client('ec2')` to access VPC, subnets, security groups, network ACLs, and endpoints.
  - Use `describe_vpcs()` to check existing VPCs and their configurations.
  - Use `describe_subnets()` to verify that subnets are appropriately isolated (e.g., private subnets).
  - Use `describe_security_groups()` to check the inbound and outbound rules for Amazon Keyspaces resources, ensuring they only allow traffic from trusted sources.
  - Use `describe_network_acls()` to ensure network ACLs are configured to allow only necessary traffic and deny unwanted traffic.
  - Use `describe_vpc_endpoints()` to ensure that the necessary VPC endpoints for Amazon Keyspaces are set up and correctly configured.

- **AWS CLI Commands:**
  - `aws ec2 describe-vpcs`
  - `aws ec2 describe-subnets`
  - `aws ec2 describe-security-groups`
  - `aws ec2 describe-network-acls`
  - `aws ec2 describe-vpc-endpoints`

- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - VPCs and their associated resources in the regions where Amazon Keyspaces is deployed.

- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Ensure VPCs and subnets are configured to isolate Amazon Keyspaces resources from the public internet.
  - Verify that security groups allow inbound access only from trusted IPs/security groups and restrict outbound traffic as required.
  - Ensure network ACLs permit only necessary protocols, ports, and IP ranges.

- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** Configurations for VPC, subnets, security groups, network ACLs, and endpoints meet the specified security requirements.
  - **Fail:** Any configuration that allows unauthorized access or does not match recommended security practices. 

These automated checks ensure that network security for Amazon Keyspaces is properly configured, addressing both connectivity and access control requirements as per the CIS benchmark.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The configurations related to VPCs, subnets, security groups, network ACLs, and VPC endpoints can indeed be verified using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI. These are all standard network configurations that can be programmatically accessed and validated.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The boto3 methods and CLI commands listed are valid for retrieving information about VPCs, subnets, security groups, network ACLs, and VPC endpoints.
- The program name `aws_vpc_network_security_keyspaces_configured` is somewhat descriptive but could be more specific regarding the exact security aspect being checked.
- The description of the security checks is accurate, but it could benefit from additional specificity regarding the exact security configurations expected for Amazon Keyspaces.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- **Program Name:** Consider renaming to `aws_vpc_keyspaces_network_security_check` to better reflect the focus on network security specific to Amazon Keyspaces.
- **Missing Considerations:** The assessment could include checks for ensuring that VPC endpoints are configured with policies that restrict access to only the necessary IAM roles or services. Additionally, consider verifying that the VPC is not using default configurations, which might be less secure.
- **Alternative AWS API Methods:** Consider using AWS Config rules to continuously monitor these configurations for compliance. AWS Config provides managed rules that can automatically check for compliance with best practices.
- **Documentation:** Include references to specific CIS benchmarks or AWS best practices to provide context for the security requirements.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded and uses appropriate AWS services and methods to automate the compliance check. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the approach aligns with AWS security best practices.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- **Modify:** Update the program name to `aws_vpc_keyspaces_network_security_check` for better clarity. Consider expanding the scope to include checks for VPC endpoint policies and non-default configurations. Additionally, integrating AWS Config for continuous compliance monitoring could enhance the robustness of the audit.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that all required network security configurations for Amazon Keyspaces—VPC selection/creation, subnet configuration, security group and network ACL rules, and VPC endpoint setup—are fully accessible via AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). These configurations are standard AWS resources with properties that can be programmatically queried and validated. No manual inspection or console-only steps are required for this control.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_vpc_keyspaces_network_security_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** VPCs, Subnets, Security Groups, Network ACLs, VPC Endpoints (with focus on Amazon Keyspaces)
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_vpcs()`
  - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_subnets()`
  - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_security_groups()`
  - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_network_acls()`
  - `boto3.client('ec2').describe_vpc_endpoints()`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - VPC exists and is not the default VPC.
  - Subnets used for Keyspaces are private (no direct route to internet gateway).
  - Security groups restrict inbound/outbound access to trusted IPs/networks only.
  - Network ACLs allow only necessary traffic and deny all others.
  - VPC endpoints for Keyspaces are present and have restrictive access policies (if applicable).
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - Amazon Keyspaces resources are deployed in a non-default VPC.
  - Only private subnets are used for Keyspaces.
  - Security groups and network ACLs do not allow unrestricted (0.0.0.0/0) access.
  - VPC endpoints for Keyspaces exist and are restricted to necessary IAM roles/services.
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - Keyspaces resources in default VPC or public subnets.
  - Security groups or ACLs allow broad/unrestricted access.
  - Missing or overly permissive VPC endpoints.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation. The second opinion suggested a more descriptive program name and additional specificity, which is incorporated in the final program name and automation details.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
